nodes.label: Save Query Result as Graph Node
nodes.norm_label: save query result as graph node

nodes.label: Research Plugin Resource Model (one file = one resource)
nodes.rationale: Deliberate MVP simplification: one repo file maps to one resource, with no artifact refs, manifests, previews, or cache dirs. Version tokens use path+mtime_ns+size_bytes, not file contents, to avoid needing an artifact store.
nodes.norm_label: research plugin resource model (one file = one resource)

nodes.label: Pre-registered Decision Rule Enforcement
nodes.rationale: Experiment reviewer must apply the plan's decision rule and success threshold to observed results, checking for goalpost-moving (reaching beyond or quietly ignoring the pre-registered rule). This guards against post-hoc rationalization.

nodes.label: Modal Sandbox Execution Model (SSH, no jobs)
nodes.norm_label: modal sandbox execution model (ssh, no jobs)

nodes.label: Sandbox Sync Contract (/workspace/synced vs /workspace/unsynced)
nodes.rationale: The provider-neutral sync contract separates synced (pulled locally by rsync) from unsynced (ephemeral remote-only) workspace directories, preventing accidental syncing of large datasets and caches.

nodes.label: MLflow Tracking Server Port 5000 in Sandbox
nodes.norm_label: mlflow tracking server port 5000 in sandbox

nodes.label: Plugin Architecture Design Thesis
nodes.rationale: The old backend had too many first-class subsystems for MVP. The plug-in architecture shrinks the implementation: Codex does reasoning/editing/local scripting, MCP owns durable memory and mutation validation. This hard boundary keeps the implementation small while preserving correctness.

nodes.label: Server-Issued Reviewer Capability Token Model
nodes.rationale: Local reviewer identity cannot rely on IP/machine boundaries. The MVP models identity as server-issued workflow capability: scoped one-time tokens tied to one target, one role, and immutable target snapshot. Not cryptographic proof of independent minds, but a practical local boundary with audit trail.

nodes.label: MCP Mutation Model (all state changes via MCP tools)
nodes.rationale: All meaning-changing mutations go through MCP tools. Codex may edit files locally, but research state is not changed until MCP accepts a mutation. This ensures the MCP server is the single source of truth and prevents race conditions.
nodes.norm_label: mcp mutation model (all state changes via mcp tools)

nodes.label: Sandbox Execution: No Job Abstraction Pattern
nodes.rationale: There is no job abstraction. The agent requests a sandbox and runs shell commands directly over SSH. This keeps the interface simple: Codex owns the command execution, MCP owns policy/state/visibility. Avoids the complexity of a job queue abstraction.
nodes.norm_label: sandbox execution: no job abstraction pattern

nodes.label: Frontend UX Rules (no guessing workflow state)
nodes.rationale: The UI must keep an explicit active project_id; the backend does not default to the first project. UI should not guess workflow state in frontend code and must show failed reviews prominently. This prevents the UI from becoming a stale or incorrect mirror of server state.
nodes.norm_label: frontend ux rules (no guessing workflow state)

nodes.label: Control/Data Plane Split Proposal
nodes.rationale: The cloud backend cannot see a user's local filesystem, so any code that reads/writes local files or spawns rsync/ssh must run locally. This single rule determines the entire control/data plane split. Direct local-to-VM SSH is simpler and cheaper than routing bytes through a cloud relay. The MCP server must stay stdlib-only and stateless so it can be spawned/killed by the agent client — hosting a long-lived sync worker there would cause sync to die with the editor and risk two workers fighting over the same folder.

nodes.label: Lease Authority (cloud-owned sync coordination)
nodes.rationale: Do not attempt peer-to-peer lease coordination between local processes. The cloud is the only thing all local clients can see, so leases must live there to safely coordinate multiple local daemons.

nodes.label: Direction Policy (per-subtree sync authority)
nodes.rationale: --delete + multi-client is a footgun; a stale client can wipe live work. Per-subtree direction policy in the sync session closes this gap.

nodes.label: SSH CA Credential Model
nodes.rationale: Prefer SSH CA: local daemon generates keypair (private key never leaves machine), cloud signs public key into short-TTL certificate scoped to one sandbox. Keeps private key local while validity/revocation/rotation belong to control plane.

nodes.label: State of Research Canvas (landing page concept)
nodes.rationale: The current UI is a faithful database viewer when it needs to be a supervisor's situational-awareness layer. A canvas-style home with active pulses makes orientation across the entire loop the primary affordance instead of entity-type lists.
nodes.norm_label: state of research canvas (landing page concept)

nodes.label: Two-Altitude UI Pattern (status-first, drill to markdown)
nodes.rationale: Do not build a UI-side summarizer; stay close to rendering the agent's markdown files directly — that is the native channel through which the agent controls what the user sees. Shape readability at the source by instructing the agent to author its markdown in prescribed structure.
nodes.norm_label: two-altitude ui pattern (status-first, drill to markdown)

nodes.label: workflow.status_and_next Tool
nodes.rationale: This tool exists because Codex may lose conversation memory. The server must be able to re-orient the agent and the user from durable state. Returns a slim projection (not the full state) because this call is polled constantly.

nodes.label: Sandbox Reaper (background expiry enforcer)
nodes.rationale: Lambda Labs VMs bill until manually killed. The reaper runs a best-effort final rsync before terminating so results survive beyond the sandbox lifetime.

nodes.label: HF_TOKEN Security Model (secrets via Modal API, not synced files)
nodes.norm_label: hf_token security model (secrets via modal api, not synced files)

nodes.label: Resource Version Token (path+mtime_ns+size_bytes)
nodes.rationale: mtime alone is weak: coarse filesystem resolution, copies can preserve timestamps, tools can rewrite content without meaningful timestamp semantics. The stable compromise combines path+mtime_ns+size_bytes plus content_sha256 to avoid duplicate version rows on re-observation without semantic change.

nodes.label: Resource Attempt Scoping (workflow gates only count current attempt)
nodes.norm_label: resource attempt scoping (workflow gates only count current attempt)

nodes.label: Reviewer Independence Boundary (capability-scoped sessions)
nodes.rationale: In a local Codex workflow, identity cannot rely on IP address, user account, or machine boundary. Checking an agent's memory is not reliable. Treat reviewer identity as workflow identity enforced via capability-scoped sessions. This creates a practical local independence boundary — not cryptographic proof — so high-risk gates can require human review.

nodes.label: Verified vs Attested vs Human Review Tiers
nodes.norm_label: verified vs attested vs human review tiers

nodes.label: Daemon-First Process Topology
nodes.rationale: One long-lived backend daemon plus a thin stdio MCP proxy spawned on demand. The daemon owns SQLite state, activity log, sandbox execution backend, and SSH rsync syncer. Both UI and MCP proxy forward through it. Daemon must start before Codex makes any tool call.

nodes.label: rsync 3.x Host Prerequisite
nodes.rationale: Apple's bundled rsync is 2.6.9 (protocol 29) and cannot transfer with sandbox's rsync 3.x — the -az stream breaks with unexpected tag / connection unexpectedly closed errors.

nodes.label: Tool I/O Analyzer (debug ring buffer in tool_calls.sqlite)
nodes.norm_label: tool i/o analyzer (debug ring buffer in tool_calls.sqlite)

nodes.label: Experiment Finite State Machine
nodes.rationale: The experiment lifecycle is intentionally a fixed loop so the human can build a reliable mental model of where an experiment is in the cycle without relearning it each time. Codex must not guess state, gate, or next step — it asks workflow.status_and_next.

nodes.label: Result Sync Gate (post-execution resource sync requirement)
nodes.norm_label: result sync gate (post-execution resource sync requirement)

nodes.label: Round 1 Implemented Fixes (resource.list filters, terminal cursor, review schema, allowed_transitions)
nodes.norm_label: round 1 implemented fixes (resource.list filters, terminal cursor, review schema, allowed_transitions)

nodes.norm_label: research plugin icon (document + checkmark + diagonal line)
